relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus finishes sending the twelve and continues teaching and proclaiming.
John hears of Messiah’s works from prison.
From confinement, John asks whether Jesus is truly the coming one.
Jesus answers by pointing to what can be heard and seen.
Sight, walking, cleansing, hearing, rising, and good news reveal the kingdom’s signature.
Blessed is the one who can receive Jesus without stumbling over him.
Jesus honors John as more than wilderness spectacle.
John is not palace softness.
He is prophet and more than prophet.
He is the messenger preparing the way.
John is great among those born of women, yet heaven’s reign opens an even deeper order.
The reign presses forward with intensity.
Law and prophets reach toward John.
He carries the Elijah pattern for those able to receive it.
Hearing must become real hearing.
This generation cannot be satisfied by either song or lament.
It refuses both dance and mourning.
John is rejected for restraint.
Jesus is rejected for table-fellowship, yet wisdom is vindicated by works.
Cities that saw power but did not realign are confronted.
Chorazin and Bethsaida are measured against cities that might have turned.
Judgment is more severe where light is resisted.
Capernaum’s exaltation is challenged.
Even Sodom becomes a mirror for refused revelation.
Jesus praises the Father for revealing to infants what the self-assured miss.
The Father’s pleasure moves through hiddenness and revelation.
Father and Son know one another in a depth that is revealed, not seized.
The burdened are invited into rest.
Jesus’ yoke teaches through gentleness and lowliness of heart.
His yoke is kind, and his burden is light.
